hawkish_phrases: labor market had already reached full employment and was projected to tighten further
an unduly slow pace in removing policy accommodation could result in an overshoot of the Committee's inflation objective
many participants thought that another increase in the target range later this year was likely to be warranted
some other participants...were more worried about upside risks to inflation arising from a labor market that had already reached full employment
the stance of monetary policy remains accommodative, thereby supporting some further strengthening in labor market conditions
dovish_phrases: inflation on a 12-month basis...running below 2 percent
some patience in removing policy accommodation while assessing trends in inflation was warranted
A few of these participants thought that no further increases in the federal funds rate were called for in the near term
their decision on whether to take such a policy action would depend importantly on whether the economic data in coming months increased their confidence that inflation was moving up
inflation developments...might reflect not only transitory factors, but also the influence of developments that could prove more persistent
summary: The FOMC voted unanimously to hold the target range at 1-1.25% while announcing October initiation of balance sheet normalization, with language indicating gradual rate increases ahead despite persistent below-target inflation and concern that convergence to 2% might take longer than previously anticipated.
